steps.step: Expand \(pv\) by substituting \(p = 3x+4\) and \(v = x+5\) and using FOIL: \((3x+4)(x+5) = 3x^2 + 15x + 4x + 20 = 3x^2 + 19x + 20\).

Add all three parts together: \((3x^2+19x+20) + (-6x-8) + (x+5)\). Combine like terms: \(x^2\) terms give \(3x^2\); \(x\) terms give \(19x - 6x + x = 14x\); constants give \(20 - 8 + 5 = 17\). Result: \(3x^2 + 14x + 17\).

• Choice A: \(3x^2 + 12x + 7\). The \(x\)-coefficient is 12, but we computed 14. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: \(3x^2 + 14x + 17\). Matches \(3x^2 + 14x + 17\) exactly. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • Choice C: \(3x^2 + 19x + 20\). This equals \(pv\) alone, without the \(-2p + v\) adjustment. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: \(3x^2 + 26x + 33\). The \(x\)-coefficient and constant are both too large; likely a sign error on \(-2p\). Eliminated.
